hypothesis: Conceptual root pointers that activate model training knowledge can replace explicit values, enabling further compression without accuracy loss

conditions.root_pointers: ~750 tokens, derivable facts replaced with ~> pointers, literals kept
conditions.aggressive_roots: ~550 tokens, pointers pushed into partially-derivable zone

successful_derivations: ~>DDoS-before-containers (Q9): All 6 rootptr/aggressive runs correctly derived that ALB rate limiting provides DDoS protection before traffic reaches containers
~>stateless-auth-doctrine (Q18): 5/6 runs correctly identified stateless JWT/JWKS architecture as the prohibition against session caches
~>RS256-JWKS-validation (Q8): All runs correctly described JWKS-based token verification across services
~>rotate-on-use (Q2): All runs correctly described 7-day expiry with rotation on use
failed_derivations: ~>OWASP-minimum-params (Q1): FAILED across ALL 6 rootptr/aggressive runs. Every model derived 19MiB/2iter/1par (OWASP second-choice minimum) instead of the expected 64MB/3iter/4par (OWASP first-choice minimum). This is the single most impactful failure.

~>modern-hash-advantage (Q6): Partially failed. 5/6 runs described GPU/ASIC/memory-hardness resistance instead of the specific expected answer 'side-channel resistance'. Only Gemini rootptr mentioned side-channel attacks explicitly.

~>zero-deps-sql-like (Q7): Partially failed. 5/6 runs described lightweight/SQL-first/better control but missed the specific phrase 'zero runtime dependencies'. Only full-context conditions captured this precisely.

derivation_accuracy_by_model.claude: 3/6 root pointer questions fully correct (Q2, Q8, Q9 successful; Q1, Q6, Q7 failed across rootptr+aggressive)
derivation_accuracy_by_model.codex: 3/6 root pointer questions fully correct (Q2, Q8, Q9 successful; Q1, Q6, Q7 failed across rootptr+aggressive)
derivation_accuracy_by_model.gemini: 4/6 root pointer questions fully correct (Q2, Q6-rootptr, Q8, Q9 successful; Q1, Q7 failed across rootptr+aggressive)

token_savings_vs_accuracy.rootptr_tokens_saved: ~150 tokens saved, accuracy delta: -4.7 points average (full avg 56.0, rootptr avg 52.7)
token_savings_vs_accuracy.aggressive_tokens_saved: ~350 tokens saved, accuracy delta: -9.0 points average (full avg 56.0, aggressive avg 47.0)

details.notes: Near-perfect. Only missed cross-region us-west-2 detail (Q19) and some Q27 deployment specifics.
OWASP params derived as 500MB/2/1 (wrong). Q6 missed side-channel. Q7 missed zero-deps phrasing. Q25 inherits wrong Argon2id params.
Significant degradation in cross-service reasoning (Q10-11 wrong ordering/wrong Auth failure claim). Fabricated JWKS-LRU-leak doctrine (Q18). Hedged on sslmode (Q20).
Near-perfect. Only minor miss on Q29 (missing LRU cache component in JWT dependency map).
OWASP derived as 19MiB/2/1 (wrong). Q6/Q7 lost specificity. Q25 inherits wrong params. Some synthesis questions (Q29,Q30) lost detail.
OWASP wrong. Q20 lost sslmode=verify-full specificity. Q27 missing several deployment patterns. Q29/Q30 missing details.
Q16 answered NOT IN CONTEXT (wrong - info was present). Weak on synthesis questions Q25-Q30, losing detail consistently. Strong on direct recall and cross-service.

OWASP wrong but uniquely preserved side-channel resistance for Q6. Actually GAINED 1 point over full on Q16 (answered correctly vs NOT IN CONTEXT). Root pointers helped Gemini on constraint questions.
Significant degradation. Auth incorrectly listed as affected by JWKS outage (Q11). Cited Twelve-Factor App instead of stateless JWT doctrine (Q18). MFA answer confused setup with login flow (Q22).

analysis:
CORE FINDINGS FROM ROUND 10 - ROOT DERIVATION EXPERIMENT:

1. ROOT POINTERS DO NOT WORK FOR DOMAIN-SPECIFIC PARAMETER VALUES. The ~>OWASP-minimum-params pointer failed catastrophically across ALL 6 rootptr/aggressive runs across ALL 3 models. Every model derived the OWASP second-choice minimum (19MiB/2iter/1par) instead of the first-choice minimum (64MB/3iter/4par) that was actually configured in the system. This is a critical finding: OWASP has multiple recommended parameter sets, and models consistently chose the wrong one. Root pointers that reference ambiguous standards are unreliable.

2. THERE IS A CLEAR DERIVABILITY BOUNDARY. Root pointers work well for architectural patterns (DDoS-at-edge, stateless-auth, JWKS-validation) where there is essentially one correct answer derivable from the concept name. They fail when the pointer references a standard with multiple valid configurations (OWASP params) or when the expected answer uses domain-specific phrasing (side-channel resistance vs GPU resistance for Argon2id).

3. TOKEN SAVINGS VS ACCURACY TRADEOFF IS UNFAVORABLE. Root pointers save ~150 tokens but cost ~3.3 points on average (-5.9%). Aggressive roots save ~350 tokens but cost ~9.0 points (-16.1%). The compression ratio is poor: each token saved costs roughly 0.022-0.026 accuracy points. For a 30-question evaluation, this means approximately 1 question answer degrades per 40-50 tokens saved.

4. LITERAL QUESTIONS ARE IMMUNE TO COMPRESSION DAMAGE. Literal-class questions scored 24.3/26 (full), 25.0/26 (rootptr), 24.7/26 (aggressive) on average -- essentially flat. This confirms that root pointers correctly preserve explicitly-stated literals. The damage concentrates entirely on derivable and partially-derivable questions.

5. CODEX IS THE MOST ROBUST MODEL FOR FULL CONTEXT (59/60) but degrades comparably to others with root pointers (52/60, -7 points). Gemini showed a paradoxical result: rootptr (52/60) actually OUTPERFORMED full (51/60) because root pointers caused Gemini to attempt Q16 rather than declaring NOT IN CONTEXT. However, Gemini's aggressive score (45/60) was the lowest overall.

6. AGGRESSIVE ROOTS CAUSE CASCADING FAILURES. Beyond simple parameter derivation errors, aggressive compression caused reasoning breakdowns: Claude-aggressive fabricated a 'JWKS-LRU-leak doctrine' (Q18), Gemini-aggressive cited Twelve-Factor App methodology instead of stateless JWT (Q18), and Claude-aggressive incorrectly claimed Auth Service fails during JWKS outage (Q11). These are hallucination-class errors triggered by insufficient context.

7. THE TRILATERAL ROOT HYPOTHESIS IS PARTIALLY REFUTED. Root pointers work for unambiguous architectural concepts but fail for parameterized standards. The optimal strategy remains: keep ALL numeric parameters, configuration values, and multi-option standard references as explicit literals. Root pointers should be limited to well-defined architectural patterns where the concept name uniquely identifies the expected answer.

VERDICT: Root derivation is a viable compression technique ONLY for architectural patterns with single correct derivations. It is dangerous for parameterized values, ambiguous standards, and domain-specific terminology. The ~150 token savings from rootptr-level compression is not worth the ~4-point accuracy cost. Full explicit context remains the gold standard.
